overall_assessment: The final-baseline package is security-acceptable for RR-AUTH-04's bounded scope. The earlier blocker-grade abuse-control gaps are closed in the authority packet through fail-closed limiter behavior, explicit subject-aware ceilings, deterministic guardrails, and focused plus full-suite rerun evidence. The remaining concerns are residual timing-uniformity and password-quality debt, but on this package they read as explicit accepted debt rather than reopened closure blockers.
recommended_path: Carry the remaining timing-uniformity and password-quality concerns into the RR-AUTH-04 acceptance ledger as explicit accepted debt with reopen triggers, then finish the pending closure-only review lanes and orchestration guards without reopening broader auth scope inside this slice.

findings.title: Reset timing-uniformity remains unproven on the final baseline
findings.rationale: The package now closes the explicit abuse-control defects, but it still does not claim a dedicated timing-uniformity contract across reset outcomes. The evidence proves generic pre-auth telemetry normalization, hashed single-use tokens, scoped cooldowns, and subject-aware ceilings, yet it does not show that invalid-account, invalid-token, and valid-token failure paths have been equalized enough to remove side-channel concern entirely. That leaves a real residual oracle risk, but the packet does not frame timing-uniformity as part of RR-AUTH-04's frozen blocker rule set.
findings.suggested_action: Record reset timing-uniformity as explicit accepted debt for RR-AUTH-04 with a reopen trigger if password-enabled public auth expands, or add a later bounded lane that proves or enforces response-time equalization.

findings.title: Canonical reset password policy still does not show breached or common-password screening
findings.rationale: The package proves shared canonical reset-password validation and short-password rejection without token consumption, which is enough to support the bounded lifecycle hardening claim. It does not show any breached-password or common-password control, so the remaining password-enabled flows can still satisfy the current minimum while accepting weak known choices. Given the package's frozen scope, this reads as broader password-policy debt rather than a blocker to RR-AUTH-04's fail-closed and abuse-control objectives.
findings.suggested_action: Keep breached/common-password screening as explicit non-blocking follow-up debt unless approval authority wants RR-AUTH-04 to absorb broader password-quality hardening.
